summary: Large unstubbing/decompilation batch across THP, fighters, game modes, stages, soundtest, player bonus logic, lbaudio, and baselib synth. The slice has no PR body or human review comments, but the diff replaces many `/// #` placeholders or disabled/nonmatching code with C, adds supporting prototypes and struct field typing, and the decomp.dev bot reported +3428 matched code bytes, +48 matched data bytes, 10 new matches, and 39 partial-match improvements.
what_changed: Added several THP decoder routines and declarations, including THPDec_8032F8D4, THPDec_80330158, THPDec_803310CC, THPDec_80331340, and THPDec_803313D0; THPFileInfo gained an x0C pointer at offset 0x0C.
Implemented THP/JFIF marker parsing, scan component bookkeeping, and decode wrappers that set GQR5/GQR6 before calling __THPDecompressiMCURow640x480 or __THPDecompressiMCURowNxN.

Filled fighter common capture/jump/grapple code in ftCo_Attack100.c, including ftCo_800D730C, fn_800D9558, fn_800D8EC8, fn_800D9930, fn_800D9CE8, fn_800DA054, fn_800DA8E4, and ftCo_CapturePulledHi_Phys.

Typed fighter data needed by the new common-fighter code: Link attrs x84/x8C/x90 became s32, Samus attrs x9C/xA4/xA8/xAC/xB4/xB8 became s32, Yoshi attrs gained x124/x128/x12C, and Captain speciallw gained x4.

Implemented a broad set of Kirby SpecialN/SpecialLw routines, including ftKb_SpecialLw1_Coll, ftKb_SpecialAirLwStart_Coll, ftKb_SpecialAirLw_Coll, ftKb_SpecialAirLwEnd_Coll, ftKb_SpecialNSpit0_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNSpit1_Anim, ftKb_EatWait_IASA, ftKb_SpecialAirNCaptureWait_IASA, and fn_800F9260.
Added C for fighter collision and dynamics helpers ftColl_8007861C and ftCo_8009E7B4.

Added or substantially improved game-mode functions for match-end results, tournament bracket animation, CSS/setup, and event mode, including fn_80161154, gm_80166378, fn_8018B090, gm_801B0DD0, gm_801B1C24, gm_801BAD70, and gm_801BDE94.
Converted grFlatzone_80218060 from disabled/decompiler-style code to active C and implemented grInishie1_801FC664, which became a full match per the bot report.
Restructured soundtest data around un_803FA258_t and added un_802FF9DC and un_802FFF2C implementations.
Added player bonus/stat logic fn_8003F654 and corrected pl_804D6470_t/plbonuslib signatures such as pl_80040AF0 returning u32 and x138 becoming u32.

Decompiled baselib synth functions HSD_SynthSFXSampleLoadCallback, HSD_Synth_8038A000, and HSD_Synth_8038ADD0, while tightening callback/static types such as driverPauseCallback and HSD_Synth_804D7768/804D776C.
Adjusted lbaudio_ax code around fn_80023254 and audio bank path/string handling; the bot still reported small unmatched regressions in related lbaudio functions.
smart_moves: Used local, offset-commented structs with padding for partially understood blobs, such as THPScanInfo, THPDecodeInfo, SfxLoadStreamNode, GmB03MatchData, gm_evinit, and gm_evspawn, instead of over-naming entire unknown layouts.
Updated headers only where the new C required concrete types, for example converting UNK_T fields to s32/u32/float-backed fields and replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with exact signatures.

Preserved compiler-sensitive control flow with explicit gotos, labels, byte-pointer arithmetic, and repeated branches in large game-mode/event functions rather than prematurely refactoring for readability.
Used matching aids visible in the diff: PAD_STACK, UNUSED locals, callback casts, a small gm_801B07E8_layer wrapper, an inline fn_800F9260_inline helper, and existing #pragma dont_inline context.
Kept MWERKS-only inline assembly fenced in THP decode wrappers and disabled clang-format around the asm so GQR setup instructions remain exact.
Carried constants and data placement explicitly where needed, such as static const Vec3 lbl_803B7510, exact SFX IDs, literal audio filenames, exact memzero sizes, and bitmask writes.
decomp_lessons: For broad unstub batches, couple function bodies with the smallest necessary header/prototype changes; many matches here depended on field types and callback signatures lining up.
When only offsets are known, local pad-based structs are safer and often sufficient for matching; promote them later only after field meanings are verified.

Fighter action code often needs cross-system evidence: item article attributes, JObj bone matrices, mpCheckAllRemap, callbacks, and dat_attr timing fields all interact in the hookshot/grapple/capture routines.
Do not assume readable simplification will preserve MWERKS codegen; this PR repeatedly keeps byte-pointer indexing, manual loops, gotos, and stack padding to improve match percentages.
External SDK code such as THP may require target-specific setup, here GQR5/GQR6 writes, and should be guarded for MWERKS while remaining compilable elsewhere.
Bot-reported partial improvements are useful but not the same as full matches; the report shows many 50-99% improvements plus a few regressions that future work should revisit.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions kept address-style project names: fn_800D9558, fn_8018B090, gm_801BAD70, THPDec_8032F8D4, HSD_Synth_8038A000, and un_802FFF2C.

Subsystem prefixes remained consistent: ftCo_ for common fighter, ftKb_ for Kirby, gm_ for game mode, grFlatzone_/grInishie1_ for stages, un_ for soundtest/menu unknowns, pl_ for player stats, lbAudioAx_ for audio library, and HSD_Synth_ for baselib synth.
Unknown fields use offset-like names and padding, such as x0C, x8CC, x8F0, unk0, pad7D, and x138, often with explicit offset comments.
Local helper struct names were descriptive but conservative: THPScanComp, THPScanInfo, THPDecodeInfo, SfxLoadStreamNode, GmB03MatchData, gm_evspawn, gm_evx10, and gm_evinit.
Known enums and canonical kinds were used where available, including FTKIND_LINK, FTKIND_CLINK, FTKIND_SAMUS, FTKIND_YOSHI, It_Kind_Kirby_MarioFire, and It_Kind_Kirby_DrMarioVitamin.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: THPDec_80331340 and THPDec_803313D0 use MWERKS inline asm to set GQR5 and GQR6 before bitstream prep and MCU-row decompression.
PAD_STACK and UNUSED locals were used in multiple fighter functions to preserve stack layout.
Small wrappers/inlines such as gm_801B07E8_layer and fn_800F9260_inline appear to steer inlining/call shape for matching.
Manual byte-pointer indexing, casts to array-pointer types, and offset constants were used in gm_1BA8, gm_1B03, lbaudio_ax, soundtest, and THP structures.
Control flow was left close to assembly/decompiler shape with labels such as loop_40 and block_41, explicit gotos, fallthrough comments, and repeated if chains.
Header return-type fixes, especially THPDec_8032F8D4 from void to s32 and HSD_Synth_8038A000 to s32, were part of matching the ABI and call sites.
review_feedback: No PR body, human review comments, or review records were present in the slice.
The only comment was the decomp.dev bot report: matched code reached 69.77% (+0.09%, +3428 bytes) and matched data reached 41.27% (+0.00%, +48 bytes).

Bot reported 10 new matches, including grInishie1_801FC664, ftKb_SpecialLw1_Coll, fn_800D9CE8, fn_800DA8E4, ftKb_SpecialAirLwEnd_Coll, fn_800DA054, ftKb_SpecialNSpit0_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNSpit1_Anim, ftCo_CapturePulledHi_Phys, and THPDec .sdata2.
Bot reported 39 improvements in unmatched items, led by fn_8018B090, fn_80161154, gm_801BAD70, gm_80166378, HSD_Synth_8038A000, and several fighter/THP/soundtest symbols.
Bot also reported 4 unmatched regressions: lbAudioAx_80028690, gm_1BA8 .sdata2, un_80300B58, and lbAudioAx_8002838C.
searchable_terms: ftSamusAttributes x9C xA4 xA8 xAC xB4 xB8
follow_up_queries: Which PR 2561 partial implementations remained below 100% and are good next targets: fn_8018B090, fn_80161154, gm_801BAD70, gm_80166378, HSD_Synth_8038A000, THPDec_8032F8D4?
Can THPScanComp, THPScanInfo, and THPDecodeInfo fields be promoted to shared headers with verified names and sizes?
Investigate bot-reported regressions in lbAudioAx_80028690, lbAudioAx_8002838C, gm_1BA8 .sdata2, and soundtest un_80300B58.
Follow up on Link/Samus/Yoshi dat_attr field names introduced here and replace x84/x9C/x124-style names when behavior is confirmed.
Review gm_804D6900 event-mode x10/xC structures for stable struct definitions after gm_801BAD70 and gm_801BDE94 are closer to matching.
Look for safe ways to deduplicate repeated Kirby SpecialLw and EatWait logic only after matching constraints are understood.
